findings.evidence: Organization missing recommended: logo, contactPoint
WebPage missing recommended: dateModified, isPartOf, primaryImageOfPage, inLanguage
FAQPage schema has 7 Questions but page shows ~16 FAQ pairs

No TL;DR block (expected

or paragraph starting with 'TL;DR:')
6/19 H2s are questions (32% — target ≥40%)
1612 words exceeds definitional maximum 1000 (target 700) — readers drop
definitional posts should have DefinedTerm schema — not present
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
39 em-dashes (6.79 per 400 words; target <1)
40 tricolons (8.71/500 words; target ≤2)
AI-signature vocabulary present: robust(1)
Specific human voice vs committee 2/10 — Reads as templated SEO glossary boilerplate with bylines, review dates, and section scaffolding typical of AI content farms.

suggestedPatch.target: This glossary guide defines AEO for 2026, explains why the lines with GEO and LLMO have disappeared, provides real audit data and brand examples, and offers a practical table to compare each approach.
suggestedPatch.before: This glossary guide defines AEO for 2026, explains why the lines with GEO and LLMO have disappeared, provides real audit data and brand examples, and offers a practical table to compare each approach.
suggestedPatch.after: Here's what I've learned auditing 1,240 SaaS brands: AEO, GEO, and LLMO aren't three things anymore. Treating them separately is burning your budget.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific human voice vs committee" scored 2/10: Reads as templated SEO glossary boilerplate with bylines, review dates, and section scaffolding typical of AI content farms.

findings.evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 4/10 — A few mild opinions ('treating them separately is wrong') but mostly generic assertions no one would disagree with.

suggestedPatch.target: AI answer engines prioritize sourceable, structured, and authoritative content.
suggestedPatch.before: AI answer engines prioritize sourceable, structured, and authoritative content.
suggestedPatch.after: Most 'AEO advice' is recycled SEO checklists with 'AI' bolted on—the actual differentiator is whether your content is extractable as a sentence.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions vs safe claims" scored 4/10: A few mild opinions ('treating them separately is wrong') but mostly generic assertions no one would disagree with.

findings.evidence: Unexpected phrasings 2/10 — Phrasing is almost entirely predictable marketing-speak: 'fragment their signal', 'dilute performance', 'holistic strategy'.

suggestedPatch.target: Brands that spread resources across separate playbooks fragment their signal and dilute performance.
suggestedPatch.before: Brands that spread resources across separate playbooks fragment their signal and dilute performance.
suggestedPatch.after: Running three playbooks for three acronyms is how you end up with three mediocre content libraries and zero citations.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 2/10: Phrasing is almost entirely predictable marketing-speak: 'fragment their signal', 'dilute performance', 'holistic strategy'.

findings.evidence: Point of view vs both-sides survey 6/10 — Does take a position (convergence is required) but repeats it without pressure-testing counterarguments.

suggestedPatch.target: Originally, Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) and Large Language Model Optimization (LLMO) emerged to describe nuances of optimizing for ChatGPT and similar LLMs.
suggestedPatch.before: Originally, Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) and Large Language Model Optimization (LLMO) emerged to describe nuances of optimizing for ChatGPT and similar LLMs.
suggestedPatch.after: GEO and LLMO were always marketing repackaging—invented to sell new services for what is essentially the same problem AEO already named.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Point of view vs both-sides survey" scored 6/10: Does take a position (convergence is required) but repeats it without pressure-testing counterarguments.

findings.evidence: Quotable sentences 2/10 — Nothing memorable; every attempted aphorism is a cliché like 'split discipline = split signal'.

suggestedPatch.target: Direct citations, not just links, are the new 'first page' in AI-driven discovery.
suggestedPatch.before: Direct citations, not just links, are the new 'first page' in AI-driven discovery.
suggestedPatch.after: In AI search, you don't rank—you get quoted, or you don't exist.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Quotable sentences" scored 2/10: Nothing memorable; every attempted aphorism is a cliché like 'split discipline = split signal'.

findings.evidence: Intro earns attention 2/10 — Opens with a dictionary definition and a bureaucratic publication header; no hook, no tension, no reason to keep reading.

suggestedPatch.target: AEO—answer engine optimization—refers to improving brand visibility in AI engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.before: AEO—answer engine optimization—refers to improving brand visibility in AI engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.after: Last month I watched a client's traffic crater 40% while their ChatGPT citations tripled. That gap—between rankings and being quoted—is what AEO actually solves.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Intro earns attention" scored 2/10: Opens with a dictionary definition and a bureaucratic publication header; no hook, no tension, no reason to keep reading.

findings.evidence: Intro earns attention 3/10 — Opens with a dry definition, then repeats nearly the same sentence twice more before saying anything interesting.

suggestedPatch.target: AEO—answer engine optimization—refers to improving brand visibility in AI engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.before: AEO—answer engine optimization—refers to improving brand visibility in AI engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.after: AEO is SEO after the blue links got eaten—you're optimizing to be quoted by ChatGPT, not clicked through Google.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Intro earns attention" scored 3/10: Opens with a dry definition, then repeats nearly the same sentence twice more before saying anything interesting.

findings.evidence: specificity 6/10 — The post has strong specificity in data points (34%, 27%, 58%, 3.2x, 1,240 brands, 11%) and named brands (Notion, Linear, Splitwise, Profound). However, several instructional passages collapse into abstract imperatives ('stop building three content farms,' 'fire the org chart,' 'split discipline = split signal') without concrete nouns, named tools, or actionable steps. The failing sentence pattern appears most clearly in the 'Why Unifying AEO, GEO, and LLMO' section where the argument is made in slogans rather than evidence.

suggestedPatch.target: Content best practices for AEO, GEO, and LLMO are no longer distinct. One schema library, one author bio, one dataset—stop building three content farms for three acronyms.
suggestedPatch.before: Content best practices for AEO, GEO, and LLMO are no longer distinct. One schema library, one author bio, one dataset—stop building three content farms for three acronyms.
suggestedPatch.after: AEO, GEO, and LLMO share the same top three schema types (DefinedTerm, FAQPage, Person sameAs). Brands like Notion manage a single structured content library—one canonical definition page, one author profile, one first-party dataset—rather than separate content farms per acronym.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Quality axis "specificity" scored 6/10: The post has strong specificity in data points (34%, 27%, 58%, 3.2x, 1,240 brands, 11%) and named brands (Notion, Linear, Splitwise, Profound). However, several instructional passages collapse into abstract imperatives ('stop building three content farms,' 'fire the org chart,' 'split discipline = split signal') without concrete nouns, named tools, or actionable steps. The failing sentence pattern appears most clearly in the 'Why Unifying AEO, GEO, and LLMO' section where the argument is made in slogans rather than evidence.

rewritesApplied.target: Split discipline = split signal and lower overall brand visibility.
rewritesApplied.before: Split discipline = split signal and lower overall brand visibility.
rewritesApplied.after: Split your discipline, split your signal—and watch three teams fight over the same schema file.
rewritesApplied.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 6/10: A few sharp lines ('content farms for three acronyms') are surrounded by generic phrases like 'divides your signal' and 'holistic strategy.'

rewritesApplied.target: Measure AEO with share-of-voice, citation frequency, and prompt-level appearance tracking.
rewritesApplied.before: Measure AEO with share-of-voice, citation frequency, and prompt-level appearance tracking.
rewritesApplied.after: Count how often AI answers mention you when you aren't in the prompt—that's AEO visibility.
rewritesApplied.rationale: Humanization axis "Quotable to a friend" scored 6/10: 'SEO after the blue links got eaten' and 'if a model can't copy-paste one of your sentences' are quotable, but most text isn't.

findings.evidence: Organization missing recommended: logo, contactPoint
WebPage missing recommended: dateModified, isPartOf, primaryImageOfPage, inLanguage
FAQPage schema has 7 Questions but page shows ~16 FAQ pairs

No TL;DR block (expected

or paragraph starting with 'TL;DR:')
6/19 H2s are questions (32% — target ≥40%)
1612 words exceeds definitional maximum 1000 (target 700) — readers drop
definitional posts should have DefinedTerm schema — not present
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
44 em-dashes (7.55 per 400 words; target <1)
39 tricolons (8.36/500 words; target ≤2)
AI-signature vocabulary present: robust(1)
Specific human voice vs committee 6/10 — Has flashes of voice ('fire the org chart') but buried in committee-style definitional paragraphs.

suggestedPatch.target: AEO, or Answer Engine Optimization, is the discipline of maximizing your brand's visibility and source citations in AI-driven engines such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.before: AEO, or Answer Engine Optimization, is the discipline of maximizing your brand's visibility and source citations in AI-driven engines such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.after: AEO is what happens when your brand gets quoted by ChatGPT instead of clicked on Google—and most teams are still optimizing for the wrong one.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific human voice vs committee" scored 6/10: Has flashes of voice ('fire the org chart') but buried in committee-style definitional paragraphs.
